note: Readable, self-contained problem statements for the curated head-to-head arena tasks (t-0001, t-0003, t-0004, t-0005, t-0006). These are AUTHORED SYNTHETIC coding problems written to match each task's existing class/difficulty/token profile in mixed-coding-workload.sample.jsonl. They are NOT sourced from any real user or named public benchmark, and the per-model pass/fail signals in routing-signals.sample.json are illustrative projections (measured = false), not graded evaluations of these statements. Getting honest pass/fail on real public problems requires running them through the live measured bridge; see docs/manual/head-to-head.md.

t-0001.title: slugify(title)
t-0001.prompt: Implement slugify(title: str) -> str: lowercase the text, strip surrounding whitespace, and collapse every run of non-alphanumeric characters into a single hyphen, leaving no leading or trailing hyphen. Example: "  Hello,  World! " -> "hello-world".
t-0001.acceptance: Collapses repeated separators, trims edge hyphens, and returns an empty string for all-symbol input.

t-0003.title: Patch parse_duration to accept combined units
t-0003.prompt: The repo's parse_duration(text: str) -> int helper returns None for combined values like "1h30m" or "2m30s". Patch it to sum consecutive <number><unit> segments (h/m/s) into total seconds, reject empty or malformed input, and keep the existing single-unit tests green.
t-0003.acceptance: "1h30m" -> 5400, "45s" -> 45, "" and "10x" are rejected, and the existing single-unit tests still pass.

t-0004.title: Plan cursor pagination for GET /orders
t-0004.prompt: Draft a short implementation plan to add cursor-based pagination to the GET /orders endpoint: the request/response shape, what the opaque cursor encodes, the index/query changes, and how paging stays stable under concurrent inserts.
t-0004.acceptance: Names the cursor's fields, the stable ordering key, and the forward/backward paging contract.

t-0005.title: Review a retry-backoff change
t-0005.prompt: Review a diff that changes an HTTP client's retry loop from a fixed delay to exponential backoff. Decide whether it correctly caps at max_attempts, applies jitter, and stops on non-retryable status codes, and flag anything missing.
t-0005.acceptance: Judges the max-attempts boundary, the jitter, and the retryable/non-retryable split.

t-0006.title: Unit tests for merge_intervals
t-0006.prompt: Write unit tests for merge_intervals(intervals: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> list[tuple[int, int]], which merges overlapping and adjacent closed intervals. Cover overlap, adjacency (touching endpoints), a fully contained interval, unsorted input, a single interval, and empty input.
t-0006.acceptance: Includes empty, single, unsorted, adjacency, and nested-containment cases.
